name: Pet Care
tagline: A settled routine is the kindest thing you can give a pet.

templates.title: New Puppy Starter
templates.blurb: Structure is how puppies learn the world is safe — mornings and evenings, every day.

steps.label: Potty break, first thing
steps.note: Same door, same spot — praise instantly

steps.label: Five-minute training game
steps.note: One cue per session — end on a win

routines.title: Puppy evenings
routines.footer: Puppies under 4 months: keep walks short and soft — their joints are still growing.

templates.title: Happy Adult Dog
templates.blurb: One solid daily loop: fuel, real exercise, and upkeep that prevents vet visits.

steps.label: Long walk or run
steps.note: At least 30 minutes — sniffing counts as enrichment

steps.label: Nail check and trim
steps.note: Clicking on the floor = too long

templates.title: Content Cat
templates.blurb: Cats thrive on predictability: same times, clean litter, real play.

steps.label: Scoop the litter box
steps.note: Daily scooping catches health changes early

steps.label: Ten minutes of real play
steps.note: Wand toys beat laser dots — let them catch it

educationPages.title: Routine is love
educationPages.body: Pets can't read calendars — predictable meals, walks and bedtimes are how they know everything is okay. Most 'behavior problems' shrink when the day becomes predictable.

educationPages.title: Feed, then rest
educationPages.body: Give dogs a quiet half hour after meals before hard exercise — running on a full stomach raises the risk of bloat, which is a genuine emergency in larger breeds.

educationPages.title: Grooming is a health check
educationPages.body: Brushing isn't just about fur on the couch. Your hands find lumps, ticks, and sore spots months before they'd otherwise be noticed. Make it calm and short, and end with a treat.

educationPages.title: Know your vet
educationPages.body: This app tracks habits — it can't diagnose. Not eating for a day, drinking much more than usual, or sudden lethargy are vet calls, not things to routine your way through.

products.name: Slow-feeder bowl
products.what: A bowl with ridges that makes your pet work for each mouthful.
products.why: Slows down gulpers — better digestion, lower bloat risk, and breakfast doubles as a puzzle.
products.order: Use for every meal if your pet inhales food.

products.name: Slicker brush
products.what: A fine-wire brush that lifts loose undercoat and small tangles.
products.why: Regular brushing means less shedding, fewer hairballs, and early discovery of skin trouble.
products.order: Every other day, after exercise when they're relaxed.
products.caution: Light pressure — the wires can scratch skin if you dig.

products.name: Flea & tick treatment
products.what: A monthly spot-on or chewable parasite preventative.
products.why: Prevention is far cheaper and kinder than treating an infestation or tick-borne illness.
products.order: Same date every month — pair it with your Saturday check.
products.caution: Dose by species and weight — dog products can be toxic to cats. Ask your vet before switching.
